title: Warm up
content: The slide introduces three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—through illustrated scenes viewed from inside a taxi. Each image depicts a driver navigating a distinct urban or scenic environment representative of that city.
key_claims: Beijing is depicted with modern skyscrapers, traditional architecture, and heavy traffic, viewed through a car's rearview mirror and dashboard camera.
Chongqing is shown with mountainous terrain, a cable car, and stacked traditional buildings, with the driver reading a book.
Hangzhou is illustrated with a lake, greenery, and a driver interacting with a smartphone and futuristic robotic technology interface.

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This is a title slide for the presentation, subtitled 'A Ride Through China.' It features images of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architectural landmarks representing different cities.
key_claims: The presentation theme is framed as a journey ('ride') through China's evolving urban landscape.
Visuals include a taxi sign, the Forbidden City, Hongyadong in Chongqing, and a pagoda likely from Hangzhou (Leifeng Pagoda).

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the structure of Session 1, which involves investigating and researching city development. It presents a three-step process flow using an isometric city illustration alongside colored chain boxes.
key_claims: The session follows a three-step chain: Local Features leads to Development Strategies, which leads to Outcomes & Positive Impact.
The activity focuses on investigating and researching urban development patterns.

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This is an interactive matching exercise slide where students connect each Chinese city (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) to its corresponding development strategy. Red lines show crossed connections indicating the correct city-strategy pairings.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with Sustainable urban development.
Chongqing is matched with Tourism & Economic growth.
Hangzhou is matched with Technology & tradition integration (融合).

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide summarizes the previous exercise by displaying the three taxi driver images again with their correct development strategy labels beneath each. It concludes with a key takeaway about China's evolving nation and localized development approaches.
key_claims: Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development, following the principle of 因地制宜 (adapting measures to local conditions).
